Here's an IDEA: The events in The (Wonderful) Wizard Of Oz can be seen as the PROPAGANDA  version (by the Wizard) to destroy Elphaba's reputation (& also through the eyes of Dorothy who do NOT have a full grasp of the problems/issues in Oz she "landed on". )

While WICKEDs 1 & 2 are the TRUE ACCOUNT of what really happened & transpired in Oz.

The Wizard Of Oz is the "hearsay or made-up version or even a child's account of events" & Wicked is how things really happened. Get it?

If you look at it that way, then the two stories do not contradict each other, but seen from a different perspective from two groups of characters (Dorothy & Glinda/Elphaba). ￼

Are people born Wicked or did they have Wickedness thrust upon them? The most important question of the entire movie/musical.
The gaslighting here is literally insane. Elphaba was an actual whistle blower and they chased her out and demonized her for it. Sounds like so many modern-day scenarios.
Sinead O'Connor comes to mind just one example

More importantly, is wicked LABELED upon them? Some people are wicked. In this case, a person is targeted and propagandized as “wicked” though she is not. And her counterpart is threatened to be “the good “ by the same. She doesn’t want to be the subject of a witch hunt, and she’s not very brave so she lets her brave friend go to do whatever while she suffers silently and quite alone. Being admired and being pushed to the public as “the good” is not loved. For fiyero to challenge her “you can’t resist” she says “who could?” But he doesn’t  understand the incredible pressure she is under. He is right to say “you know who could!” Because Elphaba is courageous and lives and fights for good. Glinda is being used and really both witches only have one true friend. When you are labeled wicked or Good it’s lonely. Everyone has good and bad in them. The Wizard and “government” are forcing these two to pick one or the other. But we know it’s more complicated than that as do the two friends. But noone else really knows, including fiyero. He doesn’t know all the pressure and pain Glinda is suffering. He just thinks she wants to be the shallow popular girl and can’t understand why she isn’t sticking up for her friend. Dude she’s not really happy she’s internally torn and everything she’s doing “for good” is being controlled. She’s not acting with her heart. She’s toeing the line, so to speak. He is correct that we know who could resist “this” because she already has resisted it and banished as a result. I don’t blame anyone but the wizards and his government for what happens to both of them. He propagated the labels good and wicked upon them. People “need a common enemy” and apparently a common “good” figure.
We’re not in Oz anymore. This is humanity and it’s messy. But there’s magic and music
